audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot priestly garment instruction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Aaron/sons, holy garments, ephod, stones of remembrance, breastpiece of judgment, Urim/Thummim, robe, plate, tunic, and dignity/beauty.

verses.restored: Bring near to you Aaron your brother and his sons with him from among children of Israel, to serve as priest to me: Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, sons of Aaron.
You shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, for glory and for beauty.
Speak to all wise of heart, whom I have filled with spirit of wisdom, and they shall make garments of Aaron to sanctify him, to serve as priest to me.
These are garments they shall make: breastpiece, ephod, robe, tunic of checker-work, turban, and sash; they shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother and his sons, to serve as priests to me.
They shall take gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine linen.
They shall make ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, work of skilled designer.
It shall have two shoulder pieces joined to its two ends, and it shall be joined.
The skillfully woven band on it, which is upon it, shall be of same work, from it: gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen.
Take two onyx stones and engrave on them names of sons of Israel,
six of their names on one stone and names of remaining six on second stone, according to their births.
Work of stone engraver, engravings of signet, you shall engrave two stones with names of sons of Israel; set around in gold filigree you shall make them.
Put two stones upon shoulder pieces of ephod, stones of remembrance for sons of Israel; Aaron shall bear their names before YHWH upon his two shoulders for remembrance.
Make gold filigree settings,
and two chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, work of cord; put corded chains upon settings.
Make breastpiece of judgment, work of skilled designer; like work of ephod you shall make it: gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen.
Square it shall be, doubled, a span its length and a span its width.
Fill in it settings of stones, four rows of stones: row of carnelian, topaz, and emerald, first row;
second row: turquoise, sapphire, and diamond/clear stone;
third row: jacinth, agate, and amethyst;
fourth row: beryl, onyx, and jasper; set in gold filigree in their settings.
The stones shall be according to names of sons of Israel, twelve according to their names, engravings of signet, each according to its name, for twelve tribes.
Make on breastpiece chains of corded work, twisted cords of pure gold.
Make on breastpiece two gold rings, and put two rings on two ends of breastpiece.
Put two gold cords on two rings at ends of breastpiece.
Two ends of two cords you shall put on two settings and put them on shoulder pieces of ephod toward its front.
Make two gold rings and put them on two ends of breastpiece on its edge that is toward inner side of ephod.
Make two gold rings and put them on two shoulder pieces of ephod below, toward its front, close to its joining, above woven band of ephod.
They shall bind breastpiece by its rings to rings of ephod with blue cord, so it is above woven band of ephod, and breastpiece not move from ephod.
Aaron shall bear names of sons of Israel in breastpiece of judgment upon his heart when he enters holy place, for remembrance before YHWH continually.

Put into breastpiece of judgment the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be upon Aaron's heart when he enters before YHWH; Aaron shall bear judgment of children of Israel upon his heart before YHWH continually.
Make robe of ephod all blue.
Its opening for head shall be in its middle; a binding shall be around opening, work of weaver, like opening of coat of mail, so it does not tear.
On its hem make pomegranates of blue, purple, and scarlet around its hem, and bells of gold between them all around.
Gold bell and pomegranate, gold bell and pomegranate, around hem of robe.
It shall be upon Aaron to minister; its sound shall be heard when he enters holy place before YHWH and when he goes out, so he does not die.
Make plate of pure gold and engrave on it, engravings of signet: Holy to YHWH.
Put it on blue cord, and it shall be on turban; toward front of turban it shall be.

It shall be upon Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall bear iniquity of holy things that children of Israel sanctify for all their holy gifts; it shall be on his forehead continually, for acceptance for them before YHWH.
Weave tunic of fine linen, make turban of fine linen, and make sash, work of embroiderer.
For sons of Aaron make tunics, sashes, and caps; make them for glory and beauty.
Put them on Aaron your brother and his sons with him; anoint them, fill their hand, and sanctify them, and they shall serve as priests to me.
Make for them linen undergarments to cover naked flesh; from loins to thighs they shall be.

They shall be upon Aaron and his sons when they enter tent of meeting or approach altar to minister in holy place, so they do not bear iniquity and die. It is age-long statute for him and his seed after him.

translation_notes: Priestly garments are for kavod/glory and tipheret/beauty, not vanity.
Wise-hearted artisans are filled with spirit of wisdom; craft is Spirit-enabled work.
Names of Israel are borne on shoulders and heart: priestly role carries people in remembrance and judgment before YHWH.
Urim/Thummim should be preserved without overexplaining ancient divinatory function in first pass.
Undergarment instruction protects bodily dignity and ritual safety.
safety_note: Priestly hierarchy must not be used to justify domination. The garments symbolize representative burden-bearing, beauty, holiness, and accountable service before YHWH.
